rationale: Evaluates if sudden bull-steepening in the 2s10s yield curve acts as a reliable exit signal for long-duration growth stocks.
expected_failure_mode: Bull steepening driven by rate-cut expectations might fuel a speculative bid in tech, defeating the exit.
stop_conditions: drift is not reproducible from point-in-time snapshots
hypothesis cannot be mapped to bounded diagnostics
Strategy goes to cash and misses more than 20% of a subsequent tech rally.
Win rate of the exit signal is below 45% in confirmed recessions.
